evidence: Only one @deprecated annotation in the entire src/ directory: persistSourceToRegistry in source-discovery.ts (zero callers — dead code)
No mixed JS/TS files — all source is TypeScript
No stale migration TODOs or FIXMEs referencing 'migrate', 'legacy', or 'remove after'
Auth cache maintains intentional backward compatibility with legacy flat format for external consumer (skills/supercolony/scripts/supercolony.ts)
Credential resolution has an explicit fallback chain (XDG -> legacy .env) that is actively used
loadPersona in llm.ts has a fallback to legacy agents/{name}/personas/{name}.md path — this path still exists on disk
publish-pipeline.ts has a 'Legacy single-attestation path' that is still in active use by event-runner.ts
CLI write-rate-limit migration to toolkit guards is complete (confirmed by commit 2f20e63)
incomplete_migration.issues_preventing_higher_score: Dead deprecated persistSourceToRegistry function. Auth cache writes both legacy flat and namespaced formats permanently (not a transition shim but a permanent compat layer). Persona loading has a fallback to legacy path that may no longer be needed.

incomplete_migration.dimension_character: The codebase has completed its major migrations cleanly. The chain-first migration is done. The write-rate-limit migration from src/lib to toolkit guards is complete. The only @deprecated symbol is dead code with zero callers. The remaining legacy patterns (auth cache compat format, credential resolution fallback, persona path fallback) are intentional backward-compatibility for external consumers and active file layouts, not stale shims. A developer would find clear, unified patterns with no confusing dual-path ambiguity.
incomplete_migration.score_rationale: Migrations are substantially complete with minimal residue. The deprecated persistSourceToRegistry is genuine dead code that should be removed, but it is a single isolated function. The auth cache legacy compat and persona fallback paths are intentional and documented. No mixed JS/TS, no stale migration TODOs. This places the score solidly above the 'mostly complete with minor legacy residue' 90 anchor, pulled down slightly by the dead deprecated function.

issues.summary: Deprecated persistSourceToRegistry has zero callers and should be deleted
evidence: Line 320: @deprecated annotation pointing to persistSourceToCatalog as replacement
grep confirms zero callers across the entire codebase — only the definition exists
V2 replacement persistSourceToCatalog is fully implemented in the same file
issues.suggestion: Delete the persistSourceToRegistry function (lines 315-347) and its associated V1 YAML registry types if no longer used elsewhere.

add.description: All 8 session phases work chain-only. SDK bridge has verifyTransaction, getHivePosts, resolvePostAuthor, publishHiveReaction. CLI write-rate-limit migrated to toolkit guards (commit 2f20e63). No dual-path confusion remains.

add.header: No mixed JS/TS or stale migration TODOs
add.description: All source files are TypeScript. Zero TODO/FIXME comments reference migration, legacy, or removal. The codebase has been cleaned of migration artifacts.

add.header: Auth cache maintains intentional legacy compat
add.description: saveAuthCache writes both legacy flat fields (token, address, expiresAt) and namespaced entries because skills/supercolony/scripts/supercolony.ts still reads the flat format. This is a documented compat layer for an external consumer, not an incomplete migration.
persistSourceToRegistry is annotated @deprecated with zero callers. Its V2 replacement persistSourceToCatalog is fully functional. This is the only deprecated symbol in the codebase.
